title: Polish, Graph Traversal, and Documentation
impact: Medium - Completes TaskScheduler integration and adds comprehensive documentation

streamingExporter.current: No progress reporting during streaming exports

documentation.target: Comprehensive guide in docs/ with examples and patterns

testCoverage.target: Integration tests for all new progress/cancellation features

tasks.title: Add Cancellation Support to GraphTraversal.findAllPaths
tasks.description: Add AbortSignal cancellation support to GraphTraversal.findAllPaths() method. This long-running DFS operation can be slow for deeply connected graphs and needs cancellation support.

implementation.purpose: Enable cancellation of path-finding operations that may take a long time in densely connected graphs.
keyDecisions: Add signal to TraversalOptions (affects all traversal methods)
Check cancellation at the start of each DFS recursion
Return partial paths found before cancellation

currentCode.signature: async findAllPaths(source: string, target: string, maxDepth: number = 5, options: TraversalOptions = {}): Promise<PathResult[]>

stepByStep.details:
Add signal field: signal?: AbortSignal;
Add onProgress field: onProgress?: (pathsFound: number) => void;
Add: import { checkAborted } from '../types/index.js';
Change return type to: Promise<AllPathsResult>

stepByStep.action: Add cancellation check in dfsAllPaths helper
stepByStep.details: At the start of dfsAllPaths function: checkAborted(opts.signal, 'findAllPaths');

stepByStep.action: Track paths explored for progress reporting
stepByStep.details:
Add counter: let pathsExplored = 0;
Increment on each path found and call: opts.onProgress?.(allPaths.length);
Wrap DFS call in try-catch. If cancelled, return: { paths: allPaths, cancelled: true, pathsExplored }

Add cancellation check at start: checkAborted(options?.signal, 'findShortestPath');
Check in BFS loop every 100 iterations.
Test cancellation returns partial paths, test progress callback is invoked

acceptanceCriteria: TraversalOptions includes signal and onProgress fields
AllPathsResult type created with paths, cancelled, pathsExplored fields
findAllPaths checks cancellation at each recursion
findAllPaths returns partial paths when cancelled
findShortestPath checks cancellation during BFS
Progress callback receives path count updates
All existing traversal tests pass (with return type updates)
New cancellation tests pass

tasks.title: Add Progress Callback to StreamingExporter
tasks.description: Add progress callback support to StreamingExporter.streamJSONL() and streamCSV() methods. This enables tracking progress during large streaming exports.

implementation.purpose: Enable progress tracking during streaming exports, showing entity/relation export progress.
keyDecisions: Create StreamOptions interface with onProgress callback
Report progress after each entity batch (every 100 entities)
Include bytes written in progress message

stepByStep.details: async streamJSONL(graph: ReadonlyKnowledgeGraph, options?: StreamOptions): Promise<StreamResult>

stepByStep.action: Add progress reporting in streamJSONL entity loop
Add progress for relation writing phase
stepByStep.details:
Before relations: options?.onProgress?.(entityCount, total, 'Writing relations');
Every 100 relations: options?.onProgress?.(entityCount + relationsWritten, total);

stepByStep.action: Add cancellation check between entity batches
stepByStep.details: Every 100 entities: if (options?.signal?.aborted) throw new Error('Export cancelled');
Same progress and cancellation pattern for CSV export
Add to interface: cancelled?: boolean;
Close stream properly on cancellation. Return partial result with cancelled: true.

stepByStep.action: Add tests for streaming progress and cancellation
stepByStep.details: Test progress callback invocation, test cancellation stops export

acceptanceCriteria: StreamOptions interface created with onProgress and signal
StreamResult includes optional 'cancelled' field
streamJSONL accepts optional StreamOptions parameter
streamCSV accepts optional StreamOptions parameter
Progress callback receives updates every 100 items
AbortSignal cancellation stops export and returns partial result
All existing streaming tests pass
New progress/cancellation tests pass

tasks.title: Create TaskScheduler Integration Guide
tasks.description: Create comprehensive documentation for the TaskScheduler utilities and the new progress/cancellation patterns used across all managers. This guide helps developers understand how to use and extend these patterns.

implementation.purpose: Provide comprehensive documentation for TaskScheduler utilities and async operation patterns.
keyDecisions: Include overview of all TaskScheduler utilities
Document async types and their usage patterns
Provide examples for each manager's progress/cancellation features
Include performance considerations and best practices

stepByStep.details: Create the file with the structure outlined in codeSnippets.documentationStructure
Explain TaskScheduler purpose, available utilities (batchProcess, withRetry, debounce, throttle, TaskQueue)
Document ProgressCallback, CancellableOptions, AsyncOperationOptions, BatchProgressOptions, CancellableResult

Document each manager's progress/cancellation capabilities with code examples:
- EntityManager.createEntities
- CompressionManager.findDuplicates/compressGraph
- IOManager.importGraph
- ArchiveManager.archiveEntities
- SemanticSearch.indexAll
- TransactionManager.commit
- GraphTraversal.findAllPaths
- StreamingExporter.streamJSONL/streamCSV

Include guidelines for:
- When to use each utility
- Progress reporting patterns
- Cancellation handling
- Event loop yielding
- Error handling with cancellation
Document performance implications of progress callbacks, batch sizes, setImmediate yielding
Update CLAUDE.md to reference the new guide in the ## Documentation section

acceptanceCriteria: docs/guides/TASK_SCHEDULER.md exists
All core utilities are documented with examples
All async types are documented with usage patterns
Each manager integration is documented with code examples
Best practices section provides actionable guidance
Performance considerations section covers overhead
CLAUDE.md updated to reference the new guide

tasks.title: Add Integration Tests for Progress and Cancellation
tasks.description: Create comprehensive integration tests that verify progress callbacks and cancellation work correctly across multiple managers in realistic scenarios.

implementation.purpose: Ensure TaskScheduler integration works correctly across all managers in realistic end-to-end scenarios.
keyDecisions: Test progress callback consistency across managers
Test cancellation behavior with real data
Test performance impact of progress reporting

stepByStep.details: Create the file with test structure for all managers
Create helpers: createTestGraph(size), collectProgress(), createAbortAfter(ms)
Test: Create 500 entities with progress tracking, verify progress increases monotonically
Test: Start creating 1000 entities, abort after 100ms, verify partial results
Test: Create graph with duplicates, run findDuplicates with progress, verify two-phase progress in compressGraph
Test: Import large JSON with progress, verify parsing and merging phases reported
Test: Import -> Create -> Compress -> Export pipeline with progress tracking throughout
Test: Compare operation time with and without progress callback, verify overhead < 5%

acceptanceCriteria: tests/integration/task-scheduler.test.ts exists
EntityManager progress and cancellation tests pass
CompressionManager progress tests pass
Combined workflow test verifies end-to-end progress tracking
Performance test verifies overhead < 20%
All tests run successfully with npm test

successCriteria: All four tasks completed with passing tests
Integration tests cover all major managers
npm test passes with all new tests
notes: Sprint 3 depends on both Sprint 1 and Sprint 2 being complete
Documentation should be comprehensive enough for new developers
Integration tests should use realistic data sizes (100-1000 items)
Performance tests should account for CI environment variability
Consider adding visual progress examples to documentation
